gauge.name: Ioway Creek at Ames Lincoln Way

impacts.statement: Water reaches the perimeter fence at Furman Aquatic Center located at 1635 13th St.
Water affects Stange Rd north of Ioway Creek as well as the Meadow Lane trailer park.
Water affects the Iowa State Lied Recreation Center.
Water affects the Maple...Willow and Larch dormitories on the Iowa State University campus.
Water affects Lincoln Way and University Ave. Water also reaches the top of the curb in front the Target store located at 320 S Duff Ave.
Water affects Lincoln Way west of the bridge over Ioway Creek.
Water affects the Scheman Building and Hilton Coliseum. S Duff Ave is closed between Ioway Creek and S 3rd St.
Water reaches the CyRide building located at the northeast corner of 1700 University Blvd.
Water affects S Duff Ave. Water also affects the Timbercreek Apartments located at 610 Ioway Creek Dr.
Water affects the Tall Timber Apartments located at 644 Ioway Creek Dr.
Water affects 6th St near Brookside Park.
Water affects the road at 6th St through the railroad underpass in the Brookside Park area.
Water affects the parking lots of apartments along S 5th St.
Water affects the S 4th St bridge.
Water affects the parking lots of apartments along Stonehaven Dr and threatens the Veenker Golf Course Shed.
Water affects lowlands around S 4th St.
Water affects W 190th St where it crosses Ioway Creek...west of the intersection with 510th St...or 2 1/2 miles southwest of Gilbert.
Water primarily affects low-lying areas near the stream.

dataAttribution.title: Observations courtesy of U.S. Geological Survey

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
